GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Trenery promises to help you future-proof your wardrobe with TENCEL", "As we continue to become increasingly more aware of the impact the fashion industry has on the environment, a number of eco-conscious brands are doing their bit to ensure they're helping you shop as sustainably as possible–and Trenery is one of them. Proud to have been incorporating the use of TENCEL's eco-friendly fibres in the fabrics its much-loved garments have been crafted from for the last decade, Trenery is committed to ensuring it implements sustainable practices across all aspects of its business, from design to supply chain, and beyond. "Sustainability has to be a key consideration for fashion right now," says Trenery's Head of Design, Jane Grimme. "At Trenery , we feel this is the only way forward and we have a moral obligation to work with more sustainable practices. We also believe that our customer is just as passionate as we are about working better and smarter." Designed in Melbourne, Trenery's chic yet sophisticated styles give shoppers the opportunity to invest in everyday luxuries that don't compromise on sustainability, with production via environmentally responsible methods. "It's so important now to make educated and considered decisions when purchasing, and that applies to both fashion and everything else in your life," Grimme tells Vogue. Made from TENCEL's sustainable, bio-based fibres, which are derived from wood and pulp from sustainably managed plantations, Trenery's beloved designs and must-have items are produced from compostable, biodegradable and renewable raw materials, meaning they can be integrated back into nature. This feature is one that Grimme shares, and is vital when building a sustainable wardrobe. "The sheer volume of clothing that is being manufactured and discarded each year is one of the biggest challenges for the fashion industry right now and at Trenery , we prefer a view of fashion that is slower and investing in style, quality, timelessness and longevity in your clothing," she says. Thanks to its eco-friendly nature, TENCEL's fibres are extremely durable, less likely to wrinkle when compared to similar fabrics, noticeably strong yet soft to the touch, lightweight, breathable and boast moisture absorption, colour vibrancy and thermal regulation. "We love TENCEL because it drapes beautifully, it takes colour really well and most importantly, it's a durable fibre, which means easy wear, easy care and a long lifespan for your garments which ultimately is one of the pillars of a sustainable wardrobe," adds Grimme.Want to learn more about TENCEL and their sustainability-driven #FeelsSoRight campaign with Trenery? Click here."
